Title: [S0-2.1.a] Design anchor schema
Body:
**Type:** Design  
**Where:** DB / Schema  
**Phase:** 2.1 Anchor Metadata System  

**Description:**  
Decide representation for `start`, `end`, and `context` in `change_proposals` (int offsets vs ranges, encoding, constraints).

**Notes:**  
- Document invariants (e.g., `0 ≤ start < end ≤ text_length`)
- Consider CRLF normalization impact on offsets
- Define how context windows are stored (byte vs char offsets)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Schema design document created in `docs/schema/anchor-design.md`
- [ ] Invariants documented with examples
- [ ] Review by 1 team member
- [ ] Ready for migration task (2.1.b)

Title: [S0-2.1.b] Add anchor fields + migrations
Body:
**Type:** DB Migration  
**Where:** `supabase/migrations`  
**Phase:** 2.1 Anchor Metadata System  

**Description:**  
Add anchor columns to `change_proposals` with forward + down migrations. Create and test both directions for safe rollback.

**Notes:**
- New rows must have non‑null anchors
- Define backfill or `NULL` rules for legacy rows
- Ensure migration runs without data loss
- Test down migration restores state

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Forward migration adds start_offset, end_offset, before_context, after_context
- [ ] Down migration tested and works safely
- [ ] No existing data corrupted
- [ ] Constraint: new proposals must have non-null anchors

Title: [S0-2.1.c] Wire anchors into TS models/zod
Body:
**Type:** Backend  
**Where:** `lib/db/`, `lib/types/`  
**Phase:** 2.1 Anchor Metadata System  

**Description:**  
Expose anchor fields in TypeScript models and zod schemas so every proposal in code has typed anchors. Ensure domain model and DB types stay in sync.

**Notes:**
- Update ChangeProposal type to include anchors
- Create zod schema for anchor validation
- Ensure tRPC queries return anchor fields
- No breaking changes to existing queries

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] ChangeProposal type updated with anchor fields
- [ ] Zod schema validates anchor shape
- [ ] tRPC routes return anchor fields
- [ ] TypeScript compilation succeeds with no errors
- [ ] Backwards-compatible with existing code

Title: [S0-2.1.d] Enforce 100% valid anchors
Body:
**Type:** Validation/Tests  
**Where:** `lib/validation/`, `tests/anchors/`  
**Phase:** 2.1 Anchor Metadata System  

**Description:**  
Add validators that reject proposals without valid anchors and tests that confirm 100% coverage of all proposals stored in DB.

**Notes:**
- Include at least one end‑to‑end test from evaluation output → stored proposal
- Test that invalid anchors throw on insert
- Verify 100% of _stored_ proposals have valid anchors

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Validator function rejects invalid anchors (missing fields, bad ranges)
- [ ] Unit tests cover at least 5 invalid cases
- [ ] End-to-end test: evaluation pipe → stored proposal with valid anchors
- [ ] Query confirms 100% of proposals in DB have non-null anchors
- [ ] All tests passing

Title: [S0-2.2.a] Define extraction contract
Body:
**Type:** Design  
**Where:** `docs/contracts/`, `schemas/`  
**Phase:** 2.2 Proposal Extraction Contract  

**Description:**  
Specify the input (evaluation artifact) and output (proposal with anchors) shapes, plus invariants (no drift, exact match). Tie explicitly to `evaluationresultv1` fields.

**Notes:**
- Map from evaluationresultv1 fields to proposal fields
- Define what "zero drift" means (byte-accurate, CRLF handling, Unicode normalization)
- Include examples with tricky cases (dialogue, em‑dashes, tabs)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Contract document created in `docs/contracts/extraction-contract.md`
- [ ] Input/output shapes specified with examples
- [ ] Invariants documented (no drift, exact match, fallback rules)
- [ ] 3+ tricky cases documented (dialogue, punctuation, Unicode)
- [ ] Review by 1 team member

Title: [S0-2.2.b] Implement extraction logic
Body:
**Type:** Backend  
**Where:** `lib/evaluation/proposals/`  
**Phase:** 2.2 Proposal Extraction Contract  

**Description:**  
Implement extraction that computes anchors against source text with character‑accurate matching. Treat whitespace, punctuation, and Unicode carefully; prefer pure functions.

**Notes:**
- Build `extractProposalsWithAnchors(evaluationResult, sourceText) → ChangeProposal[]`
- Use strict matching; no fallback or fuzzy matching
- Normalize CRLF for comparison but preserve in output
- Log anchor computation for debugging

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Function implemented in `lib/evaluation/proposals/extract.ts`
- [ ] Pure function (no side effects, deterministic)
- [ ] Throws on anchor mismatch (fail-closed)
- [ ] Handles CRLF normalization correctly
- [ ] Unit tests passing (see 2.2.c)

Title: [S0-2.2.c] Tests for 0 drift/partial captures
Body:
**Type:** Tests  
**Where:** `tests/proposals/`  
**Phase:** 2.2 Proposal Extraction Contract  

**Description:**  
Create golden tests where extracted spans are compared byte‑for‑byte to the manuscript. Include tricky cases (tabs, multi‑line dialogue, accents, em‑dashes).

**Notes:**
- At least 10 test cases covering: basic, CRLF, tabs, multi-line, Unicode, punctuation
- Each test verifies source.slice(start, end) === original_text
- Verify context windows are correct (40-char windows, normalized)
- Assert no partial or drifted captures

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] 10+ test cases in `tests/proposals/extraction.test.ts`
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Coverage: basic, CRLF, tabs, dialogue, Unicode, punctuation, edge cases
- [ ] Zero drift violations (100% matchers exact)
- [ ] Context window tests passing

Title: [S0-2.3.a] Design apply algorithm
Body:
**Type:** Design  
**Where:** `docs/algorithms/apply.md`  
**Phase:** 2.3 Location-Aware Apply  

**Description:**  
Specify primary strategy (offset + length validation + context) and failure modes. Document how failures map to failure codes for later (2.4).

**Notes:**
- Primary: validate offset range, check context on both sides, apply replacement
- Fallback: none (fail-closed, no fuzzy search)
- Call out explicit failure modes: anchor_miss, context_mismatch, overlap, duplicate
- Include idempotency requirements

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Algorithm doc created in `docs/algorithms/apply.md`
- [ ] Primary + fallback strategies specified
- [ ] Failure modes enumerated and mapped to codes
- [ ] Idempotency requirements stated
- [ ] 2–3 worked examples with offsets

Title: [S0-2.3.b] Implement deterministic apply function
Body:
**Type:** Backend  
**Where:** `lib/revision/applyEngine.ts` (or integrate into `lib/revision/apply.ts`)  
**Phase:** 2.3 Location-Aware Apply  

**Description:**  
Implement pure `(text, proposal) → newText` function using the designed algorithm. No side effects; must be idempotent when re‑applied to already‑updated text (or throw with correct failure code).

**Notes:**
- Use reverse-offset batching for multi-proposal applies
- Validate all before mutating any (preflight pattern)
- Explicit overlap + duplicate rejection
- Stale-anchor fail-closed behavior

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Function implemented and tested
- [ ] Pure function (no side effects, deterministic)
- [ ] Idempotent or explicit stale-anchor failure
- [ ] Batch apply with preflight validation
- [ ] All Phase 2.3 unit tests passing (see 2.3.c)

Title: [S0-2.3.c] Harness tests (≥99.5% success)
Body:
**Type:** Tests/Harness  
**Where:** `tests/applyEngine/` (or `tests/anchors/`)  
**Phase:** 2.3 Location-Aware Apply  

**Description:**  
Build a harness that runs many proposals against sample texts and reports success rate. Assert ≥99.5% success on valid anchors; log failures with enough detail for debugging.

**Notes:**
- At least 20 test cases covering: disjoint, adjacent, overlapping (reject), duplicates (reject), CRLF, stale-anchor fail-closed
- Run on diverse texts (dialogue, code, poetry, etc.)
- Log failure cases for inspection
- Assert success_rate ≥ 99.5% on valid anchors

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Harness created with 20+ test cases
- [ ] All valid proposals apply successfully
- [ ] Overlapping/duplicate proposals explicitly rejected
- [ ] Stale-anchor (re-apply to mutated text) fails with correct code
- [ ] Success rate ≥ 99.5% measured and reported

Title: [S0-2.4.a] Enumerate failure codes
Body:
**Type:** Design  
**Where:** `docs/errors/`, `lib/errors/`  
**Phase:** 2.4 Failure Classification  

**Description:**  
Define a closed set of failure codes (e.g., `ANCHOR_MISS`, `CONFLICT`, `PARSE_ERROR`, `INVARIANT_VIOLATION`). Include human‑readable messages and machine‑readable codes.

**Notes:**
- Map each failure mode from 2.3.a to a specific code
- Consider: anchor_miss, context_mismatch, overlap, duplicate, parse_error, invariant_violation
- Include HTTP status code mappings
- Create enum or constants file

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Failure code enum defined in `lib/errors/revisionCodes.ts`
- [ ] 6+ codes covering all 2.3 failure modes
- [ ] Human-readable messages for each code
- [ ] Machine-readable codes (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE)
- [ ] Code review by 1 team member

Title: [S0-2.4.b] Persist failure codes to revision_sessions
Body:
**Type:** Backend/DB  
**Where:** `supabase/migrations`, `lib/db/revisionSessions.ts`  
**Phase:** 2.4 Failure Classification  

**Description:**  
Extend schema and write path so every failed apply stores a specific failure code + context. Ensure existing readers tolerate new fields.

**Notes:**
- Add columns: apply_failure_code, apply_failure_context (JSON)
- Update revision_sessions table or create apply_outcome table
- Log failure code when apply fails
- Ensure backwards compatibility

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Migration adds apply_failure_code, apply_failure_context to schema
- [ ] Down migration tested
- [ ] Code path logs failure code on every apply error
- [ ] TypeScript types updated for new fields
- [ ] Existing queries still work (no breaking changes)

Title: [S0-2.4.c] Tests: 100% failures classified
Body:
**Type:** Tests  
**Where:** `tests/failures/`  
**Phase:** 2.4 Failure Classification  

**Description:**  
Add tests that simulate each failure mode and assert a non‑generic code is stored. Disallow any "UNKNOWN" or empty failure types in the apply pipeline.

**Notes:**
- Test each failure code: anchor_miss, context_mismatch, overlap, duplicate, etc.
- Assert failure_code is non-null and matches expected value
- Verify failure_context contains useful debugging info
- Assert no "UNKNOWN" or generic errors in logs

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Test file created: `tests/failures/apply-failure-codes.test.ts`
- [ ] 8+ test cases (one per failure code)
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Every failure code path tested
- [ ] Assertions confirm: code ≠ null, code matches expected, context non-empty
